   139→
   140→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   141→# Strategy Pattern – rolling window algorithms
   142→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   143→
   144→
   145→class WindowStrategy(ABC):
   146→    """Strategy interface for rolling window calculations."""
   147→
   148→    @abstractmethod
   149→    def add(self, event_ts: float, value: float) -> None:
   150→        """Add a new data point."""
   151→        raise NotImplementedError
   152→
   153→    @abstractmethod
   154→    def aggregate(self) -> Optional[Dict[str, float]]:
   155→        """Return aggregation results (mean, stdev, count)."""
   156→        raise NotImplementedError
   157→
   158→
   159→class TumblingWindow(WindowStrategy):
   160→    """
   161→    Standard fixed (non-overlapping) tumbling windows.
   162→
   163→    A new window starts immediately after the previous one completes.
   164→    """
   165→
   166→    def __init__(self, window_size: int):
   167→        self._window_size = timedelta(seconds=window_size)
   168→        self._bucket: List[float] = []
   169→        self._window_end: Optional[datetime] = None
   170→
   171→    def add(self, event_ts: float, value: float) -> None:
   172→        ts = datetime.fromtimestamp(event_ts)
   173→        if self._window_end is None:
   174→            self._window_end = ts + self._window_size
   175→
   176→        if ts >= self._window_end:
   177→            # Overflow → current window finished; reset and start new
   178→            self._bucket.clear()
   179→            self._window_end += self._window_size
   180→
   181→        self._bucket.append(value)
   182→
   183→    def aggregate(self) -> Optional[Dict[str, float]]:
   184→        if not self._bucket:
   185→            return None
   186→        return {
   187→            "count": len(self._bucket),
   188→            "mean": mean(self._bucket),
   189→            "stdev": pstdev(self._bucket) if len(self._bucket) > 1 else 0.0,
   190→        }
   191→
   192→
   193→class SlidingWindow(WindowStrategy):
   194→    """
   195→    Sliding window with overlap.
   196→
   197→    Uses a deque as ring-buffer to drop expired samples.
   198→    """
   199→
   200→    def __init__(self, window_size: int, slide_interval: int):
   201→        if slide_interval <= 0 or window_size <= 0:
   202→            raise ValueError("Window and slide sizes must be positive")
   203→
   204→        self._window_size = window_size
   205→        self._slide_interval = slide_interval
   206→        self._events: Deque[Tuple[float, float]] = deque()  # (ts, value)
   207→        self._last_slide: float = 0.0
   208→
   209→    def add(self, event_ts: float, value: float) -> None:
   210→        self._events.append((event_ts, value))
   211→        self._evict(event_ts)
   212→
   213→    def _evict(self, current_ts: float) -> None:
   214→        eviction_threshold = current_ts - self._window_size
   215→        while self._events and self._events[0][0] < eviction_threshold:
   216→            self._events.popleft()
   217→
   218→    def aggregate(self) -> Optional[Dict[str, float]]:
   219→        if not self._events:
   220→            return None
   221→
   222→        current_ts = self._events[-1][0]
   223→        if current_ts - self._last_slide < self._slide_interval:
   224→            return None  # Not time yet
   225→
   226→        self._last_slide = current_ts
   227→        values = [v for _, v in self._events]
   228→        return {
   229→            "count": len(values),
   230→            "mean": mean(values),
   231→            "stdev": pstdev(values) if len(values) > 1 else 0.0,
   232→        }
   233→
   234→
   235→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   236→# Observer Pattern – monitoring / telemetry
   237→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   238→
   239→
   240→class Monitor(ABC):
   241→    """Observer interface for monitoring back-ends."""
   242→
   243→    @abstractmethod
   244→    def on_event_processed(self) -> None:
   245→        pass
   246→
   247→    @abstractmethod
   248→    def on_aggregate_emitted(self, aggregate: Dict[str, float]) -> None:
   249→        pass
   250→
   251→    @abstractmethod
   252→    def on_exception(self, exc: Exception) -> None:
   253→        pass
   254→
   255→
   256→class LoggingMonitor(Monitor):
   257→    """Simple monitor that logs to Python logging module."""
   258→
   259→    def __init__(self, logger: logging.Logger):
   260→        self._logger = logger
   261→
   262→    def on_event_processed(self) -> None:
   263→        pass  # Noise reduction – only aggregates are logged
   264→
   265→    def on_aggregate_emitted(self, aggregate: Dict[str, float]) -> None:
   266→        self._logger.info("Aggregate emitted: %s", aggregate)
   267→
   268→    def on_exception(self, exc: Exception) -> None:
   269→        self._logger.exception("Aggregator exception: %s", exc)
   270→
   271→
   272→class PrometheusMonitor(Monitor):
   273→    """Monitor that exports metrics to Prometheus."""
   274→
   275→    _EVENT_COUNTER = Counter(
   276→        "psn_events_processed_total", "Total sentiment events processed"
   277→    )
   278→    _AGG_COUNTER = Counter(
   279→        "psn_aggregates_emitted_total", "Total sentiment aggregates emitted"
   280→    )
   281→    _AGG_MEAN = Gauge("psn_last_mean_sentiment", "Mean sentiment of last aggregate")
   282→    _AGG_COUNT = Gauge("psn_last_aggregate_count", "Sample count of last aggregate")
   283→
   284→    def on_event_processed(self) -> None:
   285→        self._EVENT_COUNTER.inc()
   286→
   287→    def on_aggregate_emitted(self, aggregate: Dict[str, float]) -> None:
   288→        self._AGG_COUNTER.inc()
   289→        self._AGG_MEAN.set(aggregate["mean"])
   290→        self._AGG_COUNT.set(aggregate["count"])
   291→
   292→    def on_exception(self, exc: Exception) -> None:
   293→        # Prometheus remote write not supported here – silently ignore
   294→        pass

   297→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   298→# Core Aggregator Service
   299→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   300→
   301→
   302→class SentimentRollingAggregator:
   303→    """
   304→    Service that bridges raw sentiment events to rolling aggregates.
   305→
   306→    This class is *framework-free* and purely contains business rules +
   307→    dependency inversion interfaces (Kafka in/out, monitoring).
   308→    """
   309→
   310→    def __init__(
   311→        self,
   312→        *,
   313→        config: ServiceConfig,
   314→        window_strategy: WindowStrategy,
   315→        monitors: Optional[List[Monitor]] = None,
   316→    ):
   317→        self._cfg = config
   318→        self._window = window_strategy
   319→        self._monitors = monitors or []
   320→        self._consumer: Optional[Consumer] = None
   321→        self._producer: Optional[Producer] = None
   322→        self._stop_event = threading.Event()
   323→        self._logger = logging.getLogger(self.__class__.__name__)
   324→
   325→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   326→    # Public API
   327→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   328→
   329→    def start(self) -> None:
   330→        self._logger.info("SentimentRollingAggregator starting…")
   331→        self._setup_kafka_clients()
   332→        self._setup_signal_handlers()
   333→        self._start_metrics_http_server()
   334→        self._run_event_loop()
   335→
   336→    def stop(self) -> None:
   337→        self._logger.info("Shutting down aggregator…")
   338→        self._stop_event.set()
   339→
   340→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   341→    # Internal helpers
   342→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   343→
   344→    def _setup_kafka_clients(self) -> None:
   345→        self._consumer = Consumer(self._cfg.kafka.consumer_conf())
   346→        self._consumer.subscribe([self._cfg.kafka.source_topic])
   347→
   348→        self._producer = Producer(self._cfg.kafka.producer_conf())
   349→
   350→    def _setup_signal_handlers(self) -> None:
   351→        def _handler(_sig, _frame):  # noqa: D401
   352→            self.stop()
   353→
   354→        signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, _handler)
   355→        signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handler)
   356→
   357→    def _start_metrics_http_server(self) -> None:
   358→        if any(isinstance(m, PrometheusMonitor) for m in self._monitors):
   359→            start_http_server(self._cfg.metrics_port)
   360→            self._logger.info("Prometheus metrics HTTP server on :%d", self._cfg.metrics_port)
   361→
   362→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   363→
   364→    def _run_event_loop(self) -> None:  # pylint: disable=too-many-locals
   365→        assert self._consumer and self._producer  # Assured by _setup_kafka_clients
   366→
   367→        flush_deadline = time.monotonic() + self._cfg.flush_interval
   368→
   369→        try:
   370→            while not self._stop_event.is_set():
   371→                msg = self._consumer.poll(0.1)
   372→                if msg is None:
   373→                    self._maybe_flush(flush_deadline)
   374→                    continue
   375→
   376→                if msg.error():  # pragma: no cover
   377→                    raise KafkaException(msg.error())
   378→
   379→                try:
   380→                    payload = json.loads(msg.value().decode("utf-8"))
   381→                    event_ts, sentiment_value = self._parse_event(payload)
   382→                except (ValueError, KeyError) as exc:
   383→                    self._notify_exception(exc)
   384→                    # Skip malformed message but commit offset
   385→                    self._consumer.commit(msg, asynchronous=False)
   386→                    continue
   387→
   388→                # Business logic
   389→                self._window.add(event_ts, sentiment_value)
   390→                self._notify_event_processed()
   391→
   392→                aggregate = self._window.aggregate()
   393→                if aggregate and aggregate["count"] >= self._cfg.min_samples:
   394→                    self._emit_aggregate(aggregate, event_ts)
   395→
   396→                # Offset management
   397→                self._consumer.commit(msg, asynchronous=False)
   398→                self._maybe_flush(flush_deadline)
   399→        except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except
   400→            self._notify_exception(exc)
   401→        finally:
   402→            self._cleanup()
   403→
   404→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   405→
   406→    def _emit_aggregate(self, aggregate: Dict[str, float], event_ts: float) -> None:
   407→        assert self._producer
   408→        aggregate_record = {
   409→            "timestamp": int(event_ts),
   410→            "aggregate": aggregate,
   411→        }
   412→        self._producer.produce(
   413→            topic=self._cfg.kafka.sink_topic,
   414→            value=json.dumps(aggregate_record).encode("utf-8"),
   415→        )
   416→        self._notify_aggregate_emitted(aggregate)
   417→
   418→    def _maybe_flush(self, deadline: float) -> None:
   419→        assert self._producer
   420→        if time.monotonic() >= deadline:
   421→            with suppress(BufferError):
   422→                self._producer.flush(0.0)
   423→            deadline = time.monotonic() + self._cfg.flush_interval
   424→
   425→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   426→
   427→    @staticmethod
   428→    def _parse_event(payload: MutableMapping[str, object]) -> Tuple[float, float]:
   429→        """
   430→        Validates and extracts timestamp + sentiment value.
   431→
   432→        Expected schema:
   433→        {
   434→            "timestamp": 1689012345,  # unix seconds
   435→            "sentiment":  0.42        # normalized float
   436→        }
   437→        """

   165→class PipelineEvent:
   166→    """
   167→    Simple value object representing a pipeline event.

   169→

   186→class PipelineObserver(ABC):
   187→    """
   188→    Observer interface for pipeline events.
   189→    """
   190→
   191→    @abstractmethod
   192→    def update(self, event: PipelineEvent) -> None:  # pragma: no cover
   193→        raise NotImplementedError
   194→
   195→
   196→class CompositeObserver(PipelineObserver):
   197→    """
   198→    Allows multiple observers to be treated as a single entity.
   199→    """
   200→
   201→    __slots__ = ("_observers",)
   202→
   203→    def __init__(self, *observers: PipelineObserver):
   204→        self._observers: List[PipelineObserver] = list(observers)
   205→
   206→    # Public API ----------------------------------------------------------------
   207→    def attach(self, observer: PipelineObserver) -> None:
   208→        self._observers.append(observer)
   209→
   210→    def detach(self, observer: PipelineObserver) -> None:
   211→        self._observers.remove(observer)
   212→
   213→    # PipelineObserver ----------------------------------------------------------
   214→    def update(self, event: PipelineEvent) -> None:
   215→        for obs in list(self._observers):  # shallow copy to support detach inside
   216→            try:
   217→                obs.update(event)
   218→            except Exception:  # pragma: no cover
   219→                LOGGER.exception("Uncaught error in observer %s", obs.__class__.__name__)
   220→
   221→
   222→class LoggingObserver(PipelineObserver):
   223→    """
   224→    Basic observer that logs every pipeline event.
   225→    """
   226→
   227→    def __init__(self, level: int = logging.DEBUG):
   228→        self._level = level
   229→
   230→    def update(self, event: PipelineEvent) -> None:
   231→        LOGGER.log(self._level, "Event: %s", event)

   239→class BackfillStrategy(ABC):

   257→class SparkBackfillStrategy(BackfillStrategy):
   258→    """
   259→    Implementation for Apache Spark execution engine.
   260→    """
   261→
   262→    def __init__(self) -> None:
   263→        # Lazy import to avoid heavy dependency when not required.
   264→        try:
   265→            from pyspark.sql import SparkSession  # type: ignore
   266→        except (ImportError, ModuleNotFoundError):  # pragma: no cover
   267→            LOGGER.warning("pyspark not available; using mock SparkSession.")
   268→            SparkSession = None  # type: ignore
   269→
   270→        self._SparkSession = SparkSession  # type: ignore
   271→
   272→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   273→    # BackfillStrategy                                                      #
   274→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   275→
   276→    def run(self, ctx: BackfillContext) -> int:
   277→        LOGGER.info("[Spark] Starting job %s from %s", ctx.job_id, ctx.config.source_uri)
   278→        if self._SparkSession is None:  # pragma: no cover
   279→            time.sleep(1)  # Simulate runtime
   280→            mock_rows = 42
   281→            LOGGER.info("[Spark] Finished mock run with %d rows", mock_rows)
   282→            return mock_rows
   283→
   284→        spark = self._SparkSession.builder.appName(f"Backfill-{ctx.job_id}").getOrCreate()
   285→        try:
   286→            df = spark.read.json(ctx.config.source_uri)
   287→            rows = df.count()
   288→            df.write.mode("append").saveAsTable(ctx.config.target_table)
   289→            LOGGER.info("[Spark] Wrote %d rows -> %s", rows, ctx.config.target_table)
   290→            return int(rows)
   291→        finally:
   292→            spark.stop()

   346→def _run_expectation_suite(ctx: BackfillContext) -> None:
   347→    """
   348→    Execute Great Expectations validation if configured.
   349→
   350→    Raises
   351→    ------
   352→    RuntimeError
   353→        If validation fails or GE is unavailable.

   355→    suite_name = ctx.config.expectation_suite_name
   356→    if suite_name is None:

   359→
   360→    if gx is None:  # pragma: no cover
   361→        raise RuntimeError(
   362→            "Great Expectations not installed; cannot run expectation suite."

   364→
   365→    LOGGER.info("Running GE suite '%s' for %s", suite_name, ctx.job_id)
   366→    context = gx.get_context()
   367→    try:
   368→        batch_request = {
   369→            "datasource_name": "pulstream_datasource",
   370→            "data_connector_name": "default_runtime_data_connector_name",
   371→            "data_asset_name": ctx.config.target_table,
   372→            "runtime_parameters": {"query": f"SELECT * FROM {ctx.config.target_table}"},
   373→            "batch_identifiers": {"default_identifier": str(uuid.uuid4())},
   374→        }
   375→        validation_result = context.run_checkpoint(  # type: ignore
   376→            checkpoint_name=suite_name,
   377→            batch_request=batch_request,
   378→        )
   379→    except Exception as err:  # pragma: no cover
   380→        LOGGER.exception("GE validation error in job %s", ctx.job_id)
   381→        raise RuntimeError("Failed Great Expectations validation") from err
   382→
   383→    if not validation_result["success"]:  # type: ignore
   384→        raise RuntimeError(f"Data validation failed for suite '{suite_name}'")

   388→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   389→# Orchestrator                                                                #
   390→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   391→
   392→
   393→class BackfillRunner:
   394→    """
   395→    High-level façade coordinating strategy selection, validation, metrics and
   396→    observer notifications.
   397→    """
   398→
   399→    _ENGINES: Dict[str, BackfillStrategy] = {
   400→        "spark": SparkBackfillStrategy(),
   401→        "beam": BeamBackfillStrategy(),
   402→    }
   403→
   404→    def __init__(self, observer: Optional[PipelineObserver] = None) -> None:
   405→        self._observer = observer or CompositeObserver(LoggingObserver())
   406→
   407→    # Public API ----------------------------------------------------------------
   408→    def run(self, config: BackfillJobConfig) -> int:
   409→        job_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
   410→        ctx = BackfillContext(job_id=job_id, config=config)
   411→
   412→        strategy = self._select_strategy(config.engine)
   413→        LOGGER.debug("Selected strategy %s for job %s", strategy.__class__.__name__, job_id)
   414→
   415→        # Metrics – status: Pending
   416→        _METRIC_JOB_STATUS.labels(config.job_name, config.engine).set(0)
   417→
   418→        self._notify("JOB_STARTED", ctx)
   419→        _METRIC_JOB_STATUS.labels(config.job_name, config.engine).set(1)  # Running
   420→
   421→        start_time = time.perf_counter()
   422→        try:
   423→            processed_rows = strategy.run(ctx)
   424→            ctx.rows_processed = processed_rows
   425→            _run_expectation_suite(ctx)
   426→
   427→            # Metrics
   428→            duration = time.perf_counter() - start_time
   429→            _METRIC_JOB_DURATION.labels(config.job_name, config.engine).observe(duration)
   430→            _METRIC_JOB_ROWS.labels(config.job_name, config.engine).inc(processed_rows)
   431→            _METRIC_JOB_STATUS.labels(config.job_name, config.engine).set(2)  # Success
   432→
   433→            self._notify("JOB_SUCCEEDED", ctx, payload={"rows": processed_rows})
   434→            LOGGER.info("%s completed successfully in %.2fs", config.job_name, duration)
   435→            return processed_rows
   436→
   437→        except Exception as exc:
   438→            duration = time.perf_counter() - start_time
   439→            _METRIC_JOB_DURATION.labels(config.job_name, config.engine).observe(duration)
   440→            _METRIC_JOB_STATUS.labels(config.job_name, config.engine).set(3)  # Failed
   441→
   442→            LOGGER.exception("Job %s failed after %.2fs: %s", config.job_name, duration, exc)
   443→            self._notify("JOB_FAILED", ctx, payload={"error": str(exc)})
   444→            raise
   445→
   446→    # Internal helpers ----------------------------------------------------------
   447→
   448→    def _notify(self, name: str, ctx: BackfillContext, payload: Any = None) -> None:
   449→        try:
   450→            event = PipelineEvent(name, ctx, payload)
   451→            self._observer.update(event)
   452→        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover
   453→            LOGGER.exception("Observer raised during %s notification", name)
   454→
   455→    @classmethod
   456→    def _select_strategy(cls, engine: str) -> BackfillStrategy:
   457→        try:
   458→            return cls._ENGINES[engine.lower()]
   459→        except KeyError as exc:
   460→            available = ", ".join(cls._ENGINES)
   461→            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported engine '{engine}'. Available: {available}") from exc
   462→

   464→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   465→# Scheduling                                                                  #
   466→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   467→
   468→
   469→class BackfillScheduler:
   470→    """
   471→    Cron-like scheduler responsible for enqueueing back-fill jobs based on
   472→    BackfillJobConfig definitions.
   473→    """
   474→
   475→    def __init__(self, runner: Optional[BackfillRunner] = None):
   476→        self._runner = runner or BackfillRunner()
   477→        self._scheduler = (
   478→            BackgroundScheduler(daemon=True) if BackgroundScheduler else None
   479→        )
   480→        self._jobs: Dict[str, BackfillJobConfig] = {}
   481→
   482→        if self._scheduler:
   483→            self._scheduler.start()
   484→            LOGGER.info("APScheduler started in background mode.")
   485→        else:
   486→            LOGGER.warning("APScheduler not available; falling back to polling loop.")
   487→
   488→        # Thread for naïve polling when APScheduler is missing
   489→        self._poll_thread: Optional[threading.Thread] = None
   490→        self._stop_event = threading.Event()
   491→
   492→    # Public API ----------------------------------------------------------------
   493→    def register(self, config: BackfillJobConfig) -> None:
   494→        """
   495→        Register a job with the scheduler.
   496→        """
   497→        if not config.schedule_cron:
   498→            raise ValueError("schedule_cron must be provided for scheduled jobs")
   499→
   500→        key = config.job_name
   501→        if key in self._jobs:
   502→            LOGGER.warning("Job %s already registered; overwriting.", key)
   503→            self.unregister(key)
   504→
   505→        self._jobs[key] = config
   506→        if self._scheduler:
   507→            self._scheduler.add_job(
   508→                func=functools.partial(self._runner.run, config),
   509→                trigger="cron",
   510→                id=f"backfill_{key}",
   511→                name=key,
   512→                **self._parse_cron(config.schedule_cron),
   513→            )
   514→            LOGGER.info("Registered job %s with APScheduler.", key)
   515→        else:
   516→            LOGGER.info("Registered job %s for naïve polling mode.", key)
   517→
   518→    def unregister(self, job_name: str) -> None:
   519→        """
   520→        Unregister (remove) a scheduled job.
   521→        """
   522→        self._jobs.pop(job_name, None)
   523→        if self._scheduler and self._scheduler.get_job(f"backfill_{job_name}"):
   524→            self._scheduler.remove_job(f"backfill_{job_name}")
   525→
   526→    def start(self) -> None:
   527→        """
   528→        Start the naïve polling loop if APScheduler is not present.
   529→        """
   530→        if self._scheduler:
   531→            LOGGER.debug("APScheduler already running; nothing to start.")
   532→            return
   533→
   534→        if self._poll_thread and self._poll_thread.is_alive():
   535→            return  # Already running
   536→
   537→        self._poll_thread = threading.Thread(
   538→            name="BackfillPollLoop", target=self._poll_loop, daemon=True
   539→        )
   540→        self._poll_thread.start()
   541→        LOGGER.info("Backfill naïve scheduler polling thread started.")
   542→
   543→    def shutdown(self) -> None:
   544→        """
   545→        Stop polling thread or APScheduler instance.
   546→        """
   547→        if self._scheduler:
   548→            self._scheduler.shutdown(wait=False)
   549→        else:
   550→            self._stop_event.set()
   551→            if self._poll_thread:
   552→                self._poll_thread.join(timeout=5)
   553→
   554→    # Internal helpers ----------------------------------------------------------
   555→    def _poll_loop(self) -> None:  # pragma: no cover
   556→        LOGGER.debug("Entering naïve polling loop.")
   557→        while not self._stop_event.is_set():
   558→            current_minute = _dt.datetime.utcnow().replace(second=0, microsecond=0)
   559→            for cfg in self._jobs.values():
   560→                if self._cron_matches(current_minute, cfg.schedule_cron):
   561→                    LOGGER.debug("Triggering job %s via naïve scheduler.", cfg.job_name)
   562→                    threading.Thread(
   563→                        target=self._runner.run, args=(cfg,), daemon=True
   564→                    ).start()
   565→            time.sleep(60)
   566→
   567→    @staticmethod
   568→    def _cron_matches(moment: _dt.datetime, cron_expr: str) -> bool:
   569→        """
   570→        Very naïve cron matcher that supports only 'minute hour * * *'.
   571→        """
   572→        try:
   573→            minute, hour, *_ = cron_expr.split()
   574→            return (
   575→                (minute == "*" or int(minute) == moment.minute)
   576→                and (hour == "*" or int(hour) == moment.hour)
   577→            )
   578→        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover
   579→            LOGGER.error("Invalid cron expression: %s", cron_expr)
   580→            return False
   581→
   582→    @staticmethod
   583→    def _parse_cron(cron_expr: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   584→        """
   585→        Convert POSIX cron string to APScheduler kwargs.
   586→        Supports 'minute hour day month day_of_week'.
   587→        """
   588→        minute, hour, day, month, dow = cron_expr.split()
   589→        return {
   590→            "minute": minute,
   591→            "hour": hour,
   592→            "day": day,
   593→            "month": month,
   594→            "day_of_week": dow,
   595→        }
   596→
   597→
   598→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   599→# CLI entry-point                                                             #
   600→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   601→
   602→def _example_cli() -> None:  # pragma: no cover
   603→    """
   604→    Example usage when the module is executed directly.
   605→    """
   606→    demo_config = BackfillJobConfig(
   607→        job_name="historical_twitter_ingest",
   608→        source_uri="s3://pulstream/stage/twitter/2023/*/*.json",
   609→        target_table="twitter_raw",
   610→        engine="spark",
   611→        schedule_cron="0 2 * * *",  # 02:00 UTC daily
   612→        expectation_suite_name="twitter_raw_suite",
   613→    )
   614→
   615→    runner = BackfillRunner()
   616→    scheduler = BackfillScheduler(runner)
   617→    scheduler.register(demo_config)
   618→    scheduler.start()
   619→
   620→    try:
   621→        # Keep the main thread alive to service background jobs
   622→        while True:
   623→            time.sleep(3600)
   624→    except KeyboardInterrupt:
   625→        LOGGER.info("Shutting down gracefully…")
   626→        scheduler.shutdown()
   627→
   628→
   629→if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover
   630→    _example_cli()
   631→```

Already running\n\n        self._poll_thread = threading.Thread(\n            name=\"BackfillPollLoop\", target=self._poll_loop, daemon=True\n        )\n        self._poll_thread.start()\n        LOGGER.info(\"Backfill naïve scheduler polling thread started.\")\n\n    def shutdown(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Stop polling thread or APScheduler instance.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self._scheduler:\n            self._scheduler.shutdown(wait=False)\n        else:\n            self._stop_event.set()\n            if self._poll_thread:\n                self._poll_thread.join(timeout=5)\n\n    # Internal helpers ----------------------------------------------------------\n    def _poll_loop(self) -> None:  # pragma: no cover\n        LOGGER.debug(\"Entering naïve polling loop.\")\n        while not self._stop_event.is_set():\n            current_minute = _dt.datetime.utcnow().replace(second=0, microsecond=0)\n            for cfg in self._jobs.values():\n                if self._cron_matches(current_minute, cfg.schedule_cron):\n                    LOGGER.debug(\"Triggering job %s via naïve scheduler.\", cfg.job_name)\n                    threading.Thread(\n                        target=self._runner.run, args=(cfg,), daemon=True\n                    ).start()\n            time.sleep(60)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _cron_matches(moment: _dt.datetime, cron_expr: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Very naïve cron matcher that supports only 'minute hour * * *'.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            minute, hour, *_ = cron_expr.split()\n            return (\n                (minute == \"*\" or int(minute) == moment.minute)\n                and (hour == \"*\" or int(hour) == moment.hour)\n            )\n        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n    \"\"\"\n    Example usage when the module is executed directly.\n    \"\"\"\n    demo_config = BackfillJobConfig(\n        job_name=\"historical_twitter_ingest\",\n        source_uri=\"s3://pulstream/stage/twitter/2023/*/*.json\",\n        target_table=\"twitter_raw\",\n        engine=\"spark\",\n        schedule_cron=\"0 2 * * *\",  # 02:00 UTC daily\n        expectation_suite_name=\"twitter_raw_suite\",\n    )\n\n    runner = BackfillRunner()\n    scheduler = BackfillScheduler(runner)\n    scheduler.register(demo_config)\n    scheduler.start()\n\n    try:\n        # Keep the main thread alive to service background jobs\n        while True:\n            time.sleep(3600)\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        LOGGER.info(\"Shutting down gracefully…\")\n        scheduler.shutdown()\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":  #      2→"""
     3→PulseStream Nexus – Utility helpers.
     4→
     5→This module intentionally contains *zero* domain-specific logic.  Instead it
     6→bundles together small, well-tested helpers that are widely re-used across the
     7→entire code-base (from micro-services to batch jobs).  The goal is to centralise
     8→generic concerns—configuration handling, retries, serialization, metrics,
     9→etc.—so that higher layers can focus exclusively on business rules.
    10→
    11→All functions/classes are pure or side-effect-free unless explicitly stated.
    12→They are designed to run in both sync and async contexts, and degrade
    13→gracefully when optional third-party dependencies (e.g. prometheus_client,
    14→sentry_sdk) are not available.
    15→"""
    16→
    17→from __future__ import annotations

    61→    _HAS_PROMETHEUS = True

    92→class _BaseSettings:
    93→    """
    94→    Fallback implementation when Pydantic is unavailable.
    95→
    96→    Only implements a tiny subset of pydantic.BaseSettings behaviour that we use
    97→    in the project (env var override + type coercion).
    98→    """
    99→
   100→    def __init_subclass__(cls, **kwargs):  # noqa: D401
   101→        super().__init_subclass__(**kwargs)
   102→        cls.__fields__: Dict[str, Any] = {
   103→            key: value for key, value in cls.__dict__.items() if not key.startswith("_")
   104→        }
   105→
   106→    def __init__(self, **kwargs: Any) -> None:  # noqa: D401
   107→        for field, default in self.__class__.__fields__.items():
   108→            env_val = os.getenv(field.upper())
   109→            value = kwargs.get(field, env_val if env_val is not None else default)
   110→            try:
   111→                # Naïve coercion
   112→                if isinstance(default, bool):
   113→                    value = str(value).lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
   114→                elif isinstance(default, int):
   115→                    value = int(value)
   116→                elif isinstance(default, float):
   117→                    value = float(value)
   118→            except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover
   119→                raise ValueError(f"Invalid env var for {field}: {value}") from exc
   120→            setattr(self, field, value)
   121→
   122→    def dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:  # noqa: D401
   123→        return {field: getattr(self, field) for field in self.__fields__}
   124→
   125→
   126→if _HAS_PYDANTIC:
   127→
   128→    class Settings(BaseSettings):  # type: ignore
   129→        """
   130→        Application-wide configuration model (pydantic powered).
   131→        """
   132→
   133→        LOG_LEVEL: str = Field("INFO", env="LOG_LEVEL")
   134→        SENTRY_DSN: Optional[str] = Field(None, env="SENTRY_DSN")
   135→        PROMETHEUS_ENABLED: bool = Field(True, env="PROMETHEUS_ENABLED")
   136→        RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS: int = Field(5, env="RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS")
   137→        RETRY_BASE_DELAY: float = Field(0.2, env="RETRY_BASE_DELAY")  # seconds
   138→        class Config:  # noqa: D401
   139→            env_prefix = ""  # we rely on explicit Field(env=...)
   140→
   141→else:
   142→
   143→    class Settings(_BaseSettings):  # type: ignore
   144→        LOG_LEVEL: str = "INFO"
   145→        SENTRY_DSN: Optional[str] = None
   146→        PROMETHEUS_ENABLED: bool = True
   147→        RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS: int = 5
   148→        RETRY_BASE_DELAY: float = 0.2
   149→
   150→
   151→settings = Settings()  # global immutable instance
   152→logging.basicConfig(level=getattr(logging, settings.LOG_LEVEL.upper(), logging.INFO))

   159→class _PulseJSONEncoder(json.JSONEncoder):
   160→    """
   161→    Custom JSONEncoder that understands `datetime` objects and UUIDs.
   162→
   163→    All datetimes are normalised to UTC ISO-8601 (`xxxxxZ`) to ensure
   164→    consistent downstream parsing (e.g. BigQuery, Spark).
   165→    """
   166→
   167→    def default(self, obj: Any) -> Any:  # noqa: D401
   168→        if isinstance(obj, (datetime, date)):
   169→            # Always convert date to ISO; for Naïve dt -> assume UTC
   170→            if isinstance(obj, datetime) and obj.tzinfo is None:
   171→                obj = obj.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
   172→            iso = obj.astimezone(timezone.utc).isoformat()
   173→            if iso.endswith("+00:00"):

   199→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   200→# Retry utilities
   201→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   202→
   203→
   204→class ExponentialBackoff:
   205→    """
   206→    Exponential back-off timer with full jitter (per AWS recommendations).
   207→
   208→    Example
   209→    -------
   210→        backoff = ExponentialBackoff(base=0.25, factor=2, max_attempts=5)
   211→        for attempt, delay in backoff:
   212→            try:
   213→                do_work()
   214→                break
   215→            except SomeError:
   216→                logger.warning("Attempt %s failed; retrying in %.2fs", attempt, delay)
   217→                await asyncio.sleep(delay)
   218→
   219→    References
   220→    ----------
   221→    - https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/architecture/exponential-backoff-and-jitter
   222→    """
   223→
   224→    def __init__(
   225→        self,
   226→        *,
   227→        base: float = 0.25,
   228→        factor: float = 2.0,
   229→        max_attempts: int = 5,
   230→        max_delay: float = 10.0,
   231→    ) -> None:
   232→        self.base = base
   233→        self.factor = factor
   234→        self.max_attempts = max_attempts
   235→        self.max_delay = max_delay
   236→
   237→    def __iter__(self) -> Iterator[Tuple[int, float]]:  # noqa: D401
   238→        delay = self.base
   239→        for attempt in range(1, self.max_attempts + 1):
   240→            yield attempt, delay if delay < self.max_delay else self.max_delay
   241→            delay = min(delay * self.factor, self.max_delay)
   242→
   243→    # Async helper --------------------------------------------------------- #
   244→    async def run_async(
   245→        self,
   246→        func: Callable[..., "Any"],
   247→        *args: Any,
   248→        fatal_exceptions: tuple[Type[BaseException], ...] = (),
   249→        **kwargs: Any,
   250→    ) -> Any:
   251→        """
   252→        Run *func* with retry and back-off.  Works for sync or async callables.
   253→        """
   254→        for attempt, delay in self:
   255→            try:
   256→                result = func(*args, **kwargs)
   257→                if asyncio.iscoroutine(result):
   258→                    result = await result
   259→                return result
   260→            except fatal_exceptions as fatal:  # pragma: no cover
   261→                logger.error("Fatal error encountered: %s", fatal, exc_info=True)
   262→                raise
   263→            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
   264→                if attempt == self.max_attempts:
   265→                    logger.error("Max attempts exceeded - raising error.")
   266→                    raise
   267→                jitter = random.uniform(0, delay)
   268→                logger.warning(
   269→                    "Retryable error (%s) on attempt %s/%s; sleeping %.3fs",
   270→                    exc,
   271→                    attempt,
   272→                    self.max_attempts,
   273→                    jitter,
   274→                )
   275→                await asyncio.sleep(jitter)
   276→
   277→
   278→def _get_backoff_kwargs(kwargs: MutableMapping[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   279→    """
   280→    Extract back-off tunables from decorator arguments or fallback to settings.
   281→    """
   282→    return {
   283→        "max_attempts": int(kwargs.pop("max_attempts", settings.RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS)),
   284→        "base": float(kwargs.pop("base", settings.RETRY_BASE_DELAY)),
   285→        **kwargs,
   286→    }
   287→
   288→
   289→def retry(  # noqa: D401
   290→    **backoff_kwargs: Any,
   291→) -> Callable[[Func], Func]:
   292→    """
   293→    Decorator to retry synchronous functions with exponential back-off.
   294→
   295→    Example
   296→    -------
   297→        @retry(max_attempts=3)
   298→        def flaky_io():
   299→            if random.random() < 0.8:
   300→                raise RuntimeError("Boom!")
   301→            return 42
   302→    """
   303→
   304→    def decorator(fn: Func) -> Func:
   305→        @wraps(fn)
   306→        def wrapper(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any):  # type: ignore[override]
   307→            backoff = ExponentialBackoff(**_get_backoff_kwargs(backoff_kwargs))
   308→            last_exc: Optional[Exception] = None
   309→            for attempt, delay in backoff:
   310→                try:
   311→                    return fn(*args, **kwargs)
   312→                except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
   313→                    last_exc = exc
   314→                    if attempt == backoff.max_attempts:
   315→                        break
   316→                    jitter = random.uniform(0, delay)
   317→                    logger.debug(
   318→                        "Attempt %s/%s failed: %s. Retrying in %.3fs",
   319→                        attempt,
   320→                        backoff.max_attempts,
   321→                        exc,
   322→                        jitter,
   323→                    )
   324→                    time.sleep(jitter)
   325→            # If we reached here, all retries exhausted
   326→            raise last_exc if last_exc else RuntimeError("Retry failed without exception")

   328→        return wrapper  # type: ignore[return-value]
   329→
   330→    return decorator
   331→
   332→
   333→def async_retry(  # noqa: D401
   334→    **backoff_kwargs: Any,
   335→) -> Callable[[Func], Func]:
   336→    """
   337→    Decorator for *async* functions supporting exponential back-off.
   338→    """
   339→
   340→    def decorator(fn: Func) -> Func:
   341→        if not asyncio.iscoroutinefunction(fn):
   342→            raise TypeError("@async_retry can only be applied to async functions")
   343→
   344→        @wraps(fn)
   345→        async def wrapper(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any):  # type: ignore[override]
   346→            backoff = ExponentialBackoff(**_get_backoff_kwargs(backoff_kwargs))

   349→                try:
   350→                    return await fn(*args, **kwargs)

   352→                    last_exc = exc
   353→                    if attempt == backoff.max_attempts:
   354→                        break

   356→                    logger.debug(
   357→                        "[async_retry] Attempt %s/%s failed: %s. Retrying in %.3fs",
   358→                        attempt,
   359→                        backoff.max_attempts,
   360→                        exc,
   361→                        jitter,
   362→                    )
   363→                    await asyncio.sleep(jitter)
   364→            raise last_exc if last_exc else RuntimeError("Async retry failed w/out exception")
   365→
   366→        return wrapper  # type: ignore[return-value]
   367→
   368→    return decorator

   371→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   372→# Metrics helpers
   373→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   374→
   375→if _HAS_PROMETHEUS and settings.PROMETHEUS_ENABLED:
   376→
   377→    _REQUEST_LATENCY: Histogram = Histogram(  # type: ignore[call-arg]
   378→        "pulse_request_latency_seconds",
   379→        "Latency of service requests.",
   380→        ["service", "method"],
   381→        buckets=(0.005, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 5),
   382→    )
   383→    _REQUEST_COUNT: Counter = Counter(  # type: ignore[call-arg]
   384→        "pulse_request_total",
   385→        "Count of service requests.",
   386→        ["service", "method", "status"],
   387→    )
   388→else:
   389→    _REQUEST_LATENCY = None
   390→    _REQUEST_COUNT = None
   391→
   392→
   393→@contextmanager
   394→def record_metrics(service: str, method: str) -> Generator[None, None, None]:
   395→    """
   396→    Context-manager to measure execution time and count success/failure.
   397→
   398→    Example
   399→    -------
   400→        with record_metrics("user-service", "fetch_user"):
   401→            call_expensive_io()
   402→    """
   403→    start = time.perf_counter()
   404→    exc: Optional[BaseException] = None
   405→    try:
   406→        yield
   407→    except BaseException as err:  # noqa: BLE001
   408→        exc = err
   409→        raise
   410→    finally:
   411→        duration = time.perf_counter() - start
   412→        if _REQUEST_LATENCY:
   413→            _REQUEST_LATENCY.labels(service, method).observe(duration)
   414→        if _REQUEST_COUNT:
   415→            status = "error" if exc else "success"
   416→            _REQUEST_COUNT.labels(service, method, status).inc()
   417→        logger.debug(
   418→            "Metrics[%s.%s] duration=%.4fs status=%s",
   419→            service,
   420→            method,
   421→            duration,
   422→            "error" if exc else "success",
   423→        )
   424→
   425→
   426→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   427→# Miscellaneous helpers
   428→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   429→
   430→
   431→def chunked(iterable: Iterable[T], size: int) -> Iterator[List[T]]:  # noqa: D401
   432→    """
   433→    Break *iterable* into fixed-size lists.
   434→
   435→    Example
   436→    -------
   437→        for page in chunked(users, 100):
   438→            process(page)
   439→    """
   440→    if size <= 0:
   441→        raise ValueError("size must be > 0")
   442→    it = iter(iterable)
   443→    while chunk := list(islice(it, size)):
   444→        yield chunk
   445→
   446→
   447→def stable_hash(value: Any, *, salt: str = "") -> str:  # noqa: D401
   448→    """
   449→    Compute a stable, short BLAKE2b hash for *value* (used for idempotency keys).
   450→    """
   451→    h = blake2b(digest_size=16, person=salt.encode("utf-8"))
   452→    h.update(json.dumps(value, cls=_PulseJSONEncoder, sort_keys=True).encode("utf-8"))
   453→    return h.hexdigest()
   454→
   455→
   456→def signal_safe_shutdown(loop: Optional[asyncio.AbstractEventLoop] = None) -> None:
   457→    """
   458→    Register SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers so that *asyncio* applications can shut
   459→    down gracefully.
   460→    """
   461→
   462→    async def _shutdown(sig: signal.Signals) -> None:  # noqa: D401
   463→        logger.info("Received signal: %s. Cancelling outstanding tasks...", sig.name)
   464→        tasks = [t for t in asyncio.all_tasks(loop) if t is not asyncio.current_task()]
   465→        [task.cancel() for task in tasks]
   466→        await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)
   467→        loop.stop()
   468→
   469→    loop = loop or asyncio.get_event_loop()
   470→    for sig in (signal.SIGTERM, signal.SIGINT):
   471→        loop.add_signal_handler(
   472→            sig,
   473→            lambda s=sig: asyncio.create_task(_shutdown(s)),  # noqa: B023
   474→        )
   475→
   476→
   477→def setup_sentry(dsn: Optional[str] = None) -> None:
   478→    """
   479→    Initialize Sentry (if available) with the current release + python version.
   480→    """
   481→    if not _HAS_SENTRY:
   482→        logger.debug("Sentry SDK not installed; skipping initialization.")
   483→        return
   484→
   485→    dsn = dsn or settings.SENTRY_DSN
   486→    if not dsn:
   487→        logger.debug("No SENTRY_DSN configured; skipping Sentry startup.")
   488→        return
   489→
   490→    sentry_sdk.init(
   491→        dsn=dsn,
   492→        traces_sample_rate=0.05,
   493→        environment=os.getenv("ENVIRONMENT", "dev"),
   494→        release=f"PulseStream@{os.getenv('GIT_COMMIT', 'local')}",
   495→    )
   496→    logger.info("Sentry initialized.")
   497→
   498→
   499→def validate_callable_signature(fn: Callable[..., Any], expected: int | None = None) -> None:
   500→    """
   501→    Ensure a callable's signature matches *expected* number of parameters.
   502→
   503→    Raises ValueError if the expectation isn't met.  Useful for pluggable
   504→    strategy validation where duck-typing isn't obvious.
   505→    """
   506→    params = signature(fn).parameters
   507→    if expected is not None and len(params) != expected:
   508→        raise ValueError(
   509→            f"Callable {fn.__name__} must accept {expected} parameters "
   510→            f"but has {len(params)} ({list(params)})"
   511→        )
   512→
   513→

Retrying in %.3fs\",\n                        attempt,\n                        backoff.max_attempts,\n                        exc,\n                        jitter,\n                    )\n                    await asyncio.sleep(jitter)\n            raise last_exc if last_exc else RuntimeError(\"Async retry failed w/out exception\")\n\n        return wrapper  # type: ignore[return-value]\n\n    return decorator\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Metrics helpers\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\nif _HAS_PROMETHEUS and settings.PROMETHEUS_ENABLED:\n\n    _REQUEST_LATENCY: Histogram = Histogram(  # type: ignore[call-arg]\n        \"pulse_request_latency_seconds\",\n        \"Latency of service requests.\",\n        [\"service\", \"method\"],\n        buckets=(0.005, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 5),\n    )\n    _REQUEST_COUNT: Counter = Counter(  # type: ignore[call-arg]\n        \"pulse_request_total\",\n        \"Count of service requests.\",\n        [\"service\", \"method\", \"status\"],\n    )\nelse:\n    _REQUEST_LATENCY = None\n    _REQUEST_COUNT = None\n\n\n@contextmanager\ndef record_metrics(service: str, method: str) -> Generator[None, None, None]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Context-manager to measure execution time and count success/failure.\n\n    Example\n    -------\n        with record_metrics(\"user-service\", \"fetch_user\"):\n            call_expensive_io()\n    \"\"\"\n    start = time.perf_counter()\n    exc: Optional[BaseException] = None\n    try:\n        yield\n    except BaseException as err:  # noqa: BLE001\n        exc = err\n        raise\n    finally:\n        duration = time.perf_counter() - start\n        if _REQUEST_LATENCY:\n            _REQUEST_LATENCY.labels(service, method).observe(duration)\n        if _REQUEST_COUNT:\n            status = \"error\" if exc else \"success\"\n            _REQUEST_COUNT.labels(service, method, status).inc()\n        logger.debug(\n            \"Metrics[%s.%s] duration=%.4fs status=%s\",\n            service,\n            method,\n            duration,\n            \"error\" if exc else \"success\",\n        )\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Miscellaneous helpers\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n\ndef chunked(iterable: Iterable[T], size: int) -> Iterator[List[T]]:  # noqa: D401\n    \"\"\"\n    Break *iterable* into fixed-size lists.\n\n    Example\n    -------\n        for page in chunked(users, 100):\n            process(page)\n    \"\"\"\n    if size <= 0:\n        raise ValueError(\"size must be > 0\")\n    it = iter(iterable)\n    while chunk := list(islice(it, size)):\n        yield chunk\n\n\ndef stable_hash(value: Any, *, salt: str = \"\") -> str:  # noqa: D401\n    \"\"\"\n    Compute a stable, short BLAKE2b hash for *value* (used for idempotency keys).\n    \"\"\"\n    h = blake2b(digest_size=16, person=salt.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n    h.update(json.dumps(value, cls=_PulseJSONEncoder, sort_keys=True).encode(\"utf-8\"))\n    return h.hexdigest()\n\n\ndef signal_safe_shutdown(loop: Optional[asyncio.AbstractEventLoop] = None) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Register SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers so that *asyncio* applications can shut\n    down gracefully.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    async def _shutdown(sig: signal.Signals) -> None:  # noqa: D401\n        logger.info(\"Received signal: %s. Cancelling outstanding tasks...\", sig.name)\n        tasks = [t for t in asyncio.all_tasks(loop) if t is not asyncio.current_task()]\n        [task.cancel() for task in tasks]\n        await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)\n        loop.stop()\n\n    loop = loop or asyncio.get_event_loop()\n    for sig in (signal.SIGTERM, signal.SIGINT):\n        loop.add_signal_handler(\n            sig,\n            lambda s=sig: asyncio.create_task(_shutdown(s)),  # noqa: B023\n        )\n\n\ndef setup_sentry(dsn: Optional[str] = None) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Initialize Sentry (if available) with the current release + python version.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not _HAS_SENTRY:\n        logger.debug(\"Sentry SDK not installed; skipping initialization.\")\n        return\n\n    dsn = dsn or settings.SENTRY_DSN\n    if not dsn:\n        logger.debug(\"No SENTRY_DSN configured; skipping Sentry startup.\")\n        return\n\n    sentry_sdk.init(\n        dsn=dsn,\n        traces_sample_rate=0.05,\n        environment=os.getenv(\"ENVIRONMENT\", \"dev\"),\n        release=f\"PulseStream@{os.getenv('GIT_COMMIT', 'local')}\",\n    )\n    logger.info(\"Sentry initialized.\")\n\n\ndef validate_callable_signature(fn: Callable[..., Any], expected: int | None = None) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Ensure a callable's signature matches *expected* number of parameters.\n\n    Raises ValueError if the expectation isn't met. 1→```python
     2→"""
     3→tests/test_main.py
     4→
     5→A self-contained PyTest suite that exercises a trimmed-down version of the
     6→`IngestSocialEventUseCase`, mimicking the Clean-Architecture entrypoint used by
     7→PulseStream Nexus.  The test doubles defined here (repository, metrics, etc.)
     8→allow the suite to run without the full production dependency graph.
     9→"""
    10→from __future__ import annotations
    11→
    12→import asyncio
    13→import sys
    14→from datetime import datetime, timezone
    15→
    16→import pytest
    17→from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, ValidationError
    18→
    19→
    20→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    21→#                             Domain Test Doubles                              #
    22→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    23→class FakeMetricsCollector:
    24→    """
    25→    Minimal replacement for a Prometheus counter gauge used in production.
    26→    """
    27→
    28→    def __init__(self) -> None:
    29→        self.value = 0
    30→
    31→    def inc(self, amount: int = 1) -> None:
    32→        """
    33→        Increment internal counter.
    34→
    35→        Parameters
    36→        ----------
    37→        amount : int, default=1
    38→            How much to increment the metric.
    39→        """
    40→        self.value += amount
    41→
    42→
    43→class InMemorySocialEventRepository:
    44→    """
    45→    In-memory repository that simulates persistence behaviour.
    46→    """
    47→
    48→    def __init__(self, *, should_fail: bool = False) -> None:
    49→        self._storage: dict[str, "SocialEvent"] = {}
    50→        self.should_fail = should_fail
    51→
    52→    async def save(self, event: "SocialEvent") -> None:
    53→        """
    54→        Persist an event or raise :class:`ConnectionError` if `should_fail` is
    55→        toggled.  Idempotency is enforced on `event.event_id`.
    56→        """
    57→        # Simulate network latency
    58→        await asyncio.sleep(0.01)
    59→
    60→        if self.should_fail:
    61→            raise ConnectionError("Synthetic repository failure")
    62→
    63→        # Naive idempotency guard
    64→        if event.event_id not in self._storage:
    65→            self._storage[event.event_id] = event
    66→
    67→    def get(self, event_id: str) -> "SocialEvent | None":
    68→        """Helper accessor used by the test-suite only."""
    69→        return self._storage.get(event_id)
    70→
    71→
    72→class SocialEvent(BaseModel):
    73→    """
    74→    Canonical schema for an individual social event.
    75→    """
    76→
    77→    event_id: str = Field(..., alias="id")
    78→    text: str
    79→    user_id: str
    80→    timestamp: datetime
    81→
    82→    class Config:
    83→        allow_population_by_field_name = True
    84→        frozen = True  # Guarantee immutability
    85→
    86→
    87→class IngestSocialEventUseCase:
    88→    """
    89→    Clean-Architecture interactor responsible for validating and persisting a
    90→    social event while collecting operational metrics.
    91→    """
    92→
    93→    MAX_RETRIES = 3
    94→    BACKOFF_SECONDS = 0.05
    95→
    96→    def __init__(
    97→        self,
    98→        repository: InMemorySocialEventRepository,
    99→        *,
   100→        success_counter: FakeMetricsCollector | None = None,
   101→        failure_counter: FakeMetricsCollector | None = None,
   102→    ) -> None:
   103→        self._repository = repository
   104→        self._metric_success = success_counter or FakeMetricsCollector()
   105→        self._metric_failure = failure_counter or FakeMetricsCollector()
   106→
   107→    async def execute(self, payload: dict) -> SocialEvent:
   108→        """
   109→        Parameters
   110→        ----------
   111→        payload : dict
   112→            Raw JSON-deserialised message coming from the streaming layer.
   113→
   114→        Returns
   115→        -------
   116→        SocialEvent
   117→            The validated, domain-level event instance.
   118→
   119→        Raises
   120→        ------
   121→        ValidationError
   122→            When the payload violates schema requirements.
   123→        ConnectionError
   124→            After exhausting all retries against the repository.
   125→        """
   126→        # 1) Schema validation
   127→        event = SocialEvent.parse_obj(payload)
   128→
   129→        # 2) Persistence with exponential back-off
   130→        for attempt in range(1, self.MAX_RETRIES + 1):
   131→            try:
   132→                await self._repository.save(event)
   133→                self._metric_success.inc()
   134→                return event
   135→            except ConnectionError:
   136→                if attempt >= self.MAX_RETRIES:
   137→                    self._metric_failure.inc()
   138→                    raise
   139→                await asyncio.sleep(self.BACKOFF_SECONDS * attempt)  # back-off
   140→
   141→
   142→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   143→#                                   Fixtures                                  #
   144→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   145→@pytest.fixture
   146→def valid_payload() -> dict:
   147→    return {
   148→        "id": "evt_123",
   149→        "text": "Hello, Nexus!",
   150→        "user_id": "user_42",
   151→        "timestamp": datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc),
   152→    }
   153→
   154→
   155→@pytest.fixture
   156→def invalid_payload() -> dict:
   157→    # Missing the mandatory "text" field.
   158→    return {
   159→        "id": "evt_bad",
   160→        "user_id": "user_42",
   161→        "timestamp": datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc),
   162→    }
   163→
   164→
   165→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   166→#                                   Tests                                     #
   167→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   168→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   169→async def test_execute_happy_path(valid_payload) -> None:
   170→    """A well-formed payload should be persisted exactly once."""
   171→    repo = InMemorySocialEventRepository()
   172→    metric_ok = FakeMetricsCollector()
   173→
   174→    use_case = IngestSocialEventUseCase(repo, success_counter=metric_ok)
   175→    event = await use_case.execute(valid_payload)
   176→
   177→    assert repo.get(event.event_id) == event
   178→    assert metric_ok.value == 1
   179→
   180→
   181→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   182→async def test_validation_error_bubbles_up(invalid_payload) -> None:
   183→    """Pydantic should reject structurally invalid events."""
   184→    repo = InMemorySocialEventRepository()
   185→    use_case = IngestSocialEventUseCase(repo)
   186→
   187→    with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
   188→        await use_case.execute(invalid_payload)
   189→
   190→    assert use_case._metric_failure.value == 1  # noqa: SLF001
   191→    # Repository never touched.
   192→    assert not repo._storage  # noqa: SLF001
   193→
   194→
   195→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   196→async def test_retry_then_success(valid_payload) -> None:
   197→    """
   198→    Repository fails once before recovering.  Use-case must retry and succeed.
   199→    """
   200→    repo = InMemorySocialEventRepository(should_fail=True)
   201→    use_case = IngestSocialEventUseCase(repo)
   202→
   203→    # First call should eventually raise after MAX_RETRIES
   204→    with pytest.raises(ConnectionError):
   205→        await use_case.execute(valid_payload)
   206→
   207→    # Flip the switch and ensure we can ingest afterwards
   208→    repo.should_fail = False
   209→    event = await use_case.execute(valid_payload)
   210→
   211→    assert repo.get(event.event_id) is not None
   212→    assert use_case._metric_success.value == 1  # noqa: SLF001
   213→
   214→
   215→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   216→async def test_idempotency_guard(valid_payload) -> None:
   217→    """
   218→    Persisting the same event twice should not create duplicates.
   219→    """
   220→    repo = InMemorySocialEventRepository()
   221→    use_case = IngestSocialEventUseCase(repo)
   222→
   223→    await use_case.execute(valid_payload)
   224→    await use_case.execute(valid_payload)
   225→
   226→    assert len(repo._storage) == 1  # noqa: SLF001
   227→
   228→
   229→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   230→#                 Dynamic module injection for integration tests              #
   231→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   232→# Allow 'import pulsestream_nexus.domain.use_cases.stream_ingestion as ...'
   233→# in other test suites by injecting the stub into sys.modules.
   234→module_name = "pulsestream_nexus.domain.use_cases.stream_ingestion"
   235→stub_module = type(sys)(module_name)
   236→stub_module.IngestSocialEventUseCase = IngestSocialEventUseCase
   237→stub_module.SocialEvent = SocialEvent
   238→sys.modules[module_name] = stub_module

Use-case must retry and succeed.\n    \"\"\"\n    repo = InMemorySocialEventRepository(should_fail=True)\n    use_case = IngestSocialEventUseCase(repo)\n\n    # First call should eventually raise after MAX_RETRIES\n    with pytest.raises(ConnectionError):\n        await use_case.execute(valid_payload)\n\n    # Flip the switch and ensure we can ingest afterwards\n    repo.should_fail = False\n    event = await use_case.execute(valid_payload)\n\n    assert repo.get(event.event_id) is not None\n    assert use_case._metric_success.value == 1  # noqa: SLF001\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_idempotency_guard(valid_payload) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Persisting the same event twice should not create duplicates.\n    \"\"\"\n    repo = InMemorySocialEventRepository()\n    use_case = IngestSocialEventUseCase(repo)\n\n    await use_case.execute(valid_payload)\n    await use_case.execute(valid_payload)\n\n    assert len(repo._storage) == 1  # noqa: SLF001\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n#                 Dynamic module injection for integration tests              #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Allow 'import pulsestream_nexus.domain.use_cases.stream_ingestion as ...'\n# in other test suites by injecting the stub into sys.modules.\nmodule_name = \"pulsestream_nexus.domain.use_cases.stream_ingestion\"\nstub_module = type(sys)(module_name)\nstub_module.IngestSocialEventUseCase = IngestSocialEventUseCase\nstub_module.SocialEvent = SocialEvent\nsys.modules[module_name] = stub_module\n```", "numLines": 239, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 239}}

1→```python
     2→"""
     3→tests/test_utils.py
     4→
     5→Integration-style unit-tests for the low-level utility helpers that power
     6→PulseStream Nexus.  These helpers live in the `nexus.utils.*` namespace
     7→and are intentionally kept framework-agnostic to guarantee they remain
     8→re-usable across the ingestion, transformation, and serving layers.
     9→
    10→The tests below use `pytest` and `hypothesis` for property-based checks,
    11→and they self-degrade to xfail/skip whenever an optional third-party
    12→dependency (or the module under test itself) cannot be imported.  This
    13→allows the overall test-suite to stay green in constrained CI settings
    14→(e.g. documentation builds) while still providing strong guarantees in
    15→full environments.
    16→
    17→To keep tests hermetic, slow or I/O-heavy calls (e.g., `time.sleep`,
    18→Kafka clients) are patched out with `unittest.mock`.
    19→"""
    20→
    21→from __future__ import annotations
    22→
    23→import math
    24→import re
    25→import sys
    26→import time
    27→from contextlib import nullcontext
    28→from typing import Any, Dict, Iterable, List
    29→
    30→import pytest
    31→
    32→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    33→# Optional/third-party test dependencies
    34→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    35→hypothesis = pytest.importorskip("hypothesis", reason="property-based tests")
    36→from hypothesis import given, settings
    37→from hypothesis import strategies as st
    38→
    39→# `freezegun` is handy but optional.
    40→freezegun = pytest.importorskip("freezegun", reason="requires `freezegun`")
    41→from freezegun import freeze_time
    42→
    43→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    44→# System under test
    45→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    46→#
    47→# The Clean Architecture layout places pure helpers in `nexus.utils.*`.
    48→# The try/except keeps this test-file importable even when only a subset
    49→# of the project is installed (e.g., docs builds, static analysis, etc.).
    50→#
    51→try:
    52→    from nexus.utils.backoff import exponential_backoff
    53→    from nexus.utils.kafka import topic_for_platform
    54→    from nexus.utils.schema import ValidationError, validate_event
    55→    from nexus.utils.time import calculate_throughput
    56→except ModuleNotFoundError:
    57→    # Tell pytest that these tests should be marked as xfailed when the utils
    58→    # are not present.  The parametrized fixture below will handle the skip.
    59→    exponential_backoff = topic_for_platform = validate_event = calculate_throughput = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
    60→    ValidationError = RuntimeError  # type: ignore[assignment]
    61→
    62→
    63→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    64→# Helper fixtures & markers
    65→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    66→def _require_utils():
    67→    """Skip the calling test if the target utils package is unavailable."""
    68→    if exponential_backoff is None:
    69→        pytest.skip("`nexus.utils` helpers not importable in this environment.")
    70→
    71→
    72→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    73→# Tests for `nexus.utils.backoff.exponential_backoff`
    74→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    75→@given(
    76→    retries=st.integers(min_value=0, max_value=10),
    77→    base_delay=st.floats(min_value=0.001, max_value=0.5, allow_nan=False),
    78→    factor=st.floats(min_value=1.5, max_value=3.0, allow_nan=False),
    79→)
    80→@settings(deadline=None)
    81→def test_exponential_backoff_monotonically_increases(retries: int, base_delay: float, factor: float) -> None:
    82→    """
    83→    The backoff sequence must be strictly monotonically increasing and never
    84→    drop below the `base_delay`.
    85→    """
    86→    _require_utils()
    87→
    88→    delays: List[float] = list(exponential_backoff(retries, base_delay, factor))
    89→
    90→    # Sequence length check
    91→    assert len(delays) == max(1, retries + 1)
    92→
    93→    # Monotonicity & lower-bound assertions
    94→    for previous, current in zip(delays, delays[1:]):
    95→        assert current >= previous >= base_delay
    96→
    97→    # Ensure exponential growth ≈ base_delay * factor ** idx (rounded for jitter)
    98→    # We allow a 10% margin due to optional jitter in implementation.
    99→    for idx, delay in enumerate(delays):
   100→        expected = base_delay * (factor**idx)
   101→        assert math.isclose(delay, expected, rel_tol=0.10), f"idx={idx}, expected≈{expected}, got={delay}"
   102→
   103→
   104→def test_exponential_backoff_max_sleep_time(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
   105→    """
   106→    The helper must never sleep longer than the computed delay and should handle
   107→    interrupting exceptions gracefully.
   108→    """
   109→    _require_utils()
   110→
   111→    called: List[float] = []
   112→
   113→    def fake_sleep(duration: float) -> None:  # pragma: no cover
   114→        called.append(duration)
   115→        # Simulate early keyboard interrupt half-way through the retries
   116→        if len(called) == 2:
   117→            raise KeyboardInterrupt
   118→
   119→    monkeypatch.setattr(time, "sleep", fake_sleep)
   120→
   121→    # The utility should propagate KeyboardInterrupt to the caller
   122→    with pytest.raises(KeyboardInterrupt):
   123→        # Use small retry-count to keep test fast.
   124→        list(exponential_backoff(retries=3, base_delay=0.01, factor=2.0, perform_sleep=True))
   125→
   126→    # Sleep must be invoked exactly len(called) == 2 times before abort
   127→    assert len(called) == 2
   128→    # All recorded sleep durations must match the generated backoff sequence
   129→    assert called[0] < called[1]
   130→
   131→
   132→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   133→# Tests for `nexus.utils.schema.validate_event`
   134→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   135→@freeze_time("2024-06-01 00:00:00")
   136→@pytest.mark.parametrize(
   137→    "payload",
   138→    [
   139→        # Happy-path minimal event
   140→        {
   141→            "id": "evt_123",
   142→            "platform": "twitter",
   143→            "created_at": "2024-06-01T00:00:00Z",
   144→            "payload": {"text": "The cake is a lie."},
   145→        },
   146→        # Extra metadata is allowed
   147→        {
   148→            "id": "evt_123",
   149→            "platform": "discord",
   150→            "created_at": "2024-06-01T00:00:00Z",
   151→            "payload": {"message": "Hello, world!"},
   152→            "meta": {"lang": "en"},
   153→        },
   154→    ],
   155→)
   156→def test_validate_event_happy_path(payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   157→    """
   158→    A valid event must return the canonicalized event with `created_at`
   159→    converted to a `datetime` object (implementation detail), and it must not
   160→    raise an error.
   161→    """
   162→    _require_utils()
   163→
   164→    result = validate_event(payload)
   165→    assert result["id"] == payload["id"]
   166→    assert "created_at" in result and hasattr(result["created_at"], "isoformat")
   167→
   168→
   169→invalid_event_strat = st.fixed_dictionaries(
   170→    {
   171→        # Force at least one of the required keys to be absent
   172→        "id": st.one_of(st.none(), st.text()),
   173→        "platform": st.one_of(st.none(), st.text()),
   174→        "created_at": st.one_of(st.none(), st.text()),
   175→        "payload": st.one_of(st.none(), st.dictionaries(st.text(), st.text())),
   176→    }
   177→).filter(
   178→    lambda d: any(d[k] is None for k in ("id", "platform", "created_at", "payload"))
   179→)
   180→
   181→
   182→@given(invalid_event=invalid_event_strat)
   183→@settings(deadline=None, max_examples=30)
   184→def test_validate_event_rejects_missing_required_fields(invalid_event: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   185→    """
   186→    Missing or null required fields must raise `ValidationError`.
   187→    """
   188→    _require_utils()
   189→
   190→    with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
   191→        validate_event(invalid_event)
   192→
   193→
   194→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   195→# Tests for `nexus.utils.time.calculate_throughput`
   196→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   197→def test_calculate_throughput_zero_elapsed_time() -> None:
   198→    """
   199→    Division by zero guard: when `elapsed_ms` is zero the function should
   200→    return `float('inf')` or a domain-specific sentinel such as `None`.
   201→    """
   202→    _require_utils()
   203→
   204→    result = calculate_throughput(event_count=1_000, elapsed_ms=0.0)
   205→    assert result in (float("inf"), None)
   206→
   207→
   208→@pytest.mark.parametrize(
   209→    "event_count,elapsed_ms,expected",
   210→    [
   211→        (1_000, 1_000, 1_000.0),
   212→        (5_000, 2_000, 2_500.0),
   213→        (0, 50, 0.0),
   214→    ],
   215→)
   216→def test_calculate_throughput_basic(event_count: int, elapsed_ms: float, expected: float) -> None:
   217→    """
   218→    Basic deterministic checks for the throughput helper.
   219→    """
   220→    _require_utils()
   221→
   222→    assert calculate_throughput(event_count, elapsed_ms) == expected
   223→
   224→
   225→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   226→# Tests for `nexus.utils.kafka.topic_for_platform`
   227→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   228→@pytest.mark.parametrize(
   229→    "platform,expected_regex",
   230→    [
   231→        ("twitter", r"^xnxs\.twitter\.v\d+$"),
   232→        ("reddit", r"^xnxs\.reddit\.v\d+$"),
   233→        ("mastodon", r"^xnxs\.mastodon\.v\d+$"),
   234→        ("discord", r"^xnxs\.discord\.v\d+$"),
   235→    ],
   236→)
   237→def test_topic_for_platform_naming_convention(platform: str, expected_regex: str) -> None:
   238→    """
   239→    Topic names must conform to `xnxs.<platform>.v<version>` to guarantee
   240→    compatibility with the central schema registry.
   241→    """
   242→    _require_utils()
   243→
   244→    topic_name = topic_for_platform(platform)
   245→    assert re.match(expected_regex, topic_name), f"topic `{topic_name}` did not match `{expected_regex}`"
   246→
   247→
   248→def test_topic_for_platform_unknown_platform() -> None:
   249→    """
   250→    An unknown platform should raise a `KeyError` (or a library-specific
   251→    exception) to prevent silently emitting to an invalid topic.
   252→    """
   253→    _require_utils()
   254→
   255→    with pytest.raises(KeyError):
   256→        topic_for_platform("myspace")  # vintage FTW 🤘
   257→
   258→
   259→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   260→# Main block for `python -m pytest` debugging sessions
   261→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   262→if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover
   263→    # Allow developers to quickly run this single test-file with
   264→    # `python tests/test_utils.py` while still leveraging pytest's discovery.
   265→    sys.exit(pytest.main([__file__]))
   266→```

   185→class RecordValidationError(RuntimeError):
   186→    """Raised when a record fails the expectation suite."""
   187→
   188→
   189→class RecordValidator:
   190→    """
   191→    Lightweight Great-Expectations wrapper.
   192→
   193→    The actual GE dependency is optional – if not available we fall back to a
   194→    JSON schema defined by the provided expectation suite file.
   195→    """
   196→
   197→    def __init__(self, suite_path: str) -> None:
   198→        self._logger = logging.getLogger(self.__class__.__name__)
   199→        self._rules = self._load_suite(suite_path)
   200→
   201→    @staticmethod
   202→    def _load_suite(path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   203→        if not os.path.isfile(path):
   204→            raise FileNotFoundError(f"Expectation suite not found: {path}")
   205→        with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as fp:
   206→            return json.load(fp)
   207→
   208→    def validate(self, record: EventRecord) -> None:
   209→        """
   210→        Perform very simple validation against the JSON-defined rules.
   211→
   212→        The schema might look like:
   213→        {
   214→          "required": ["id", "text", "source"],
   215→          "properties": {
   216→              "id": {"type": "string"},
   217→              "text": {"type": "string"},
   218→              "source": {"enum": ["twitter", "reddit", "mastodon", "discord"]}
   219→          }
   220→        }
   221→        """
   222→        required = set(self._rules.get("required", []))
   223→        missing = required - record.keys()
   224→        if missing:
   225→            raise RecordValidationError(f"Missing fields: {sorted(missing)}")
   226→
   227→        props = self._rules.get("properties", {})
   228→        for key, rule in props.items():
   229→            if key not in record:
   230→                continue
   231→            value = record[key]
   232→            if "enum" in rule and value not in rule["enum"]:
   233→                raise RecordValidationError(f"Invalid value for {key}: {value!r}")
   234→            if rule.get("type") == "string" and not isinstance(value, str):
   235→                raise RecordValidationError(
   236→                    f"Expected string for {key}, got {type(value).__name__}"
   237→                )
   238→
   239→
   240→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   241→# Observer Pattern – event bus                                                #
   242→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

   358→        return
   359→
   360→    if gx is None:  # pragma: no cover
   361→        raise RuntimeError(

   363→        )
   364→
   365→    LOGGER.info("Running GE suite '%s' for %s", suite_name, ctx.job_id)
   366→    context = gx.get_context()

   371→            "data_asset_name": ctx.config.target_table,
   372→            "runtime_parameters": {"query": f"SELECT * FROM {ctx.config.target_table}"},
   373→            "batch_identifiers": {"default_identifier": str(uuid.uuid4())},

   375→        validation_result = context.run_checkpoint(  # type: ignore

   378→        )

   380→        LOGGER.exception("GE validation error in job %s", ctx.job_id)
   381→        raise RuntimeError("Failed Great Expectations validation") from err
   382→
   383→    if not validation_result["success"]:  # type: ignore
